text: Song "Let's All Go To The Lobby" plays as cartoon characters march down to the lobby.
The characters sing about popcorn while the popcorn dispenser is going wild.
There are people eating candy and drinking beverages while the aforementioned characters sing about it.
The camera pans away from the concessions aisle of the theater as the characters conclude the song "Let's All Go To The Lobby."
The characters hold that last note as a green screen and counter for the movie counts down from 3 to 1 and the movie starts.
The title "Catch Me If You Can" fades in with white text over a black background.
The title "Catch Me If You Can" fades out. The title "Inspired by a true story" appears in white text with black outlines over television snow.

The snow and title fade out to a game of "To Tell The Truth" with the title "To Tell The Truth" at the bottom center of the screen and a wide shot of the entire beige and sky blue studio of the game with four people and a host in the leftmost corner. The host introduces the guests with some game show music.
Change in camera angle and the camera pans to the door with three robots opening up and three people in suits walking out. The host is giving some backstory to the three men in dark blue suits.
The camera closes up on "Frank William Abagnale" and the man gives his name. Narrator announces the guest.
Narrator announces the guest. The next man says his name is also Frank William Abagnale as the camera passes to the next man.
Narrator announces the guest. The next man says his name is also Frank William Abagnale as the camera pans over.
The camera pans over to the first Frank. The host states that from 1964-1967, the real Frank impersonated an airline pilot for Pan Am Airways. He flew over 2 million miles for free.
The camera pans over to the second Frank. The host states that he was a chief resident pediatrician at the Georgia Hospital during that time.
The camera pans over to the third Frank. The host states that the real Frank was assistant Attorney General for Louisiana.

The camera pans out to all three of them. The host states that when he was caught, the real Frank was the youngest and most daring con-man in US history. He cashed 4 million dollars in fraudulent checks, in 26 foreign countries and all 50 states. This was all before his 19th birthday.
All three men walk to their left to a beige and sky blue game show desk labelled "1","2", and "3."
The men proceed to sit down at each number to applause. There are cartoon robots behind them and on the desks. The studio's color palette is beige and sky blue.
The host introduces the first woman to ask the question, whose name is Kiddy.
The camera pans over to the first elderly woman asking the first question, which is "Why not use your talent for a legitimate profession?"
The camera closes in one Frank number 1. The man states that it was the easiest way to get money.
The camera pans over to Frank number 2 who is asked who caught him by someone who is fascinated by him. Frank number 2 answers that his name was Carl Hanratty.

The scene changes to several men standing in the rain with an umbrella, all but one with a police uniform on and a man with glasses. Screen tint is blue. The man in glasses is saying "Carl Hanratty," but the police are having trouble pronouncing the name.
The police are French it appears, saying "Oui," and the man in glasses, played by Tom Hanks, is Carl Hanratty, an FBI Agent.
Hanratty is asking the police that he has the authority to see someone named Abagnale.
The shot pans out to the entire scene, where it is still raining, the men are in the middle of a road, and on front of a prison, with the title "Marseille, France Christmas Eve 1969" in white text.

The scene changes to inside the prison, where Hanratty and an official walk up to a door, with the camera in the keyhole. The official opens the door with his key and the camera pulls back to eye level with Hanratty
Including Hanratty, three men enter the halls of the prison which is grungy and run down.
As the men walk down the hall, the front-most man picks up a stool from the side.
They stop at the end of the hallway.
The official tells Hanratty to sit on the chair he's pointing at.
The official tells Hanratty not to open the door or pass anything through to Abagnale.
Hanratty sits down and looks inside the hole, camera being behind the bars facing the FBI Agent.
Hanratty notices the sick prisoner who is wrapped in cloth and coughing with long blondish hair.
Hanratty opens up his umbrella due to some dripping, and the camera is behind the watching official.
Hanratty sits down and looks inside again and the prisoner is still coughing. Hanratty states that he has a "little bit of a cold himself."
Hanratty states that he will read "article 1"
Hanratty reads "article 1" in his Bostonian accent/Northern accent, while the prisoner is still coughing.
Hanratty looks inside the cell at Abagnale.
Hanratty tells Abagnale to stop it, and Abagnale asks for help.
Hanratty states that he will not be fooled by the "sick prisoner" routine.
Abagnale is continually coughing hard.
Hanratty read article 2 in a fast-paced Boston accent.
Frank falls over in his cell.
Hanratty notices the fallen over Frank.
Hanratty says "Frank!" and then says it again, but to no response.
Hanratty gets up mad and yells at the official to open the door and also that he needs to get a doctor.
Scene changes to Hanratty and police taking Frank to the jail hospital bed.
Hanratty tries to reassure Frank even if he is incapacitated as they place him on the bed.
The official walk back out and washes his hands with the prison guards and police.
Hanratty asks the official what he's doing in not getting a doctor and that Frank needs to be on a plane back to America the next day.
Hanratty gets agitated and tells the official to move or else he [Hanratty] will hold him responsible [the official].
They look back and see that Frank has escaped and the door that he opened is about to close, also clearing curtains that he isn't in the room.
The official yells to other guards to find him, and Hanratty has a tired/disappointed look on his face.
With the camera sweeping down, there are prisoners banging cups on the prison bars in applause at Frank's escape, who is running down the halls coughing.
Frank falls down, still coughing.
Frank gets back up and starts running
Frank falls down again, still coughing.
Frank crawls away as the officials in the prison approach from behind.
The guards catch up to Frank by walking, including Hanratty.
Frank stops and sits after the main official runs up and points a gun at Frank's head.
Frank stops and sits down for a moment as the guards catch up.
Frank looks up at the official and falls on his back on the floor.
Hanratty walks up around Frank as Frank asks for a call.
Frank says "let's go home" as the camera pulls out from him on the floor over top everyone else.
The title "The Record" fades in and expands in white text on a black screen.
The title "The Record" fades out over a black screen.
The camera pans into an animated scene with a teenage girl in a room in a tall building.
The camera is inside the apartment, and the girl opens the pizza box and grabs a pizza.
The girl, before she can eat, hears a knock on the door.
The girl opens the door and looks around to see no one is there.
The girl looks down and sees a package for her in an envelope at her doorstep.
The girl goes back to her chair and to her pizza and opens the envelope.
The girl pulls out a package with a disc inside it that says "A Single Life."
The girl pulls out the record disc from the package and the title "A film by: Job, Joris, and Marieke"
The girl gets up and puts the record disc into the record player and puts down the needle.
The song on the disc plays and she sits down and begins to eat her pizza.
The girl is about to eat the pizza but then there is a flash.
She stops and then the song plays its lyrics on the song. Part of the pizza is gone.
She notices the pizza is eaten and then looks at the disc.
The camera pans to the disc playing on the record player, and the disc is spinning on the player.
The girl stops the disc and there's a record scratch.
The girl pulls the disc back and forth on the player and pizza disappears and reappears as she tests it back and forth
The girl pulls the disc forth and the pizza pie disappears completely in the box as well as in her hand. Then she pulls it back and the pizza reappears.
The girl realizes her power and gets up then lifts the needle on the record player.
The disc plays the song and then the flash goes to her as a pregnant woman.
The woman stops the record player and stops the song from playing.
The woman pulls the disc forward and back and sees the baby develop and devolve like the pizza before.
The woman pulls the disc forward and the baby develops and pops into her arms but the baby starts crying.
The woman then flashes into her childhood self and looks at herself.
The girl goes up to record player and tries to stop it but pops off the needle
The girl flashes to her in a wheelchair as an elderly woman and she looks at herself.
The woman rolls up her wheelchair but she flashes back to the same scene over and over again, getting frustrated.
The woman rolls up again and flashes but then stops rolling up and finds that nothing happens. She then tries to roll really fast but falls back.
The woman gets up and she is an old woman who needs a walker and is wearing glasses.
The woman sees that the song is about to end and tries to get to the record player.
The woman turns into ashes in a pot in the same nursery home and the record player stops with the needle lifting.
The title "The Boyfriend" fades in with white text and expands.
The title "The Boyfriend fades out over the black background but then each letter for "high maintenance" pops up in randomized order with a twinkling song.

We see the back of a man's head at a dinner table with another woman dining on their food as the camera revolves. The man is blonde-haired and the woman is a brunette in a black dress. The table is white and they have silver silverware.
The woman is chewing on her food while the man is cutting his steak with a knife, both silent.
The woman is eating at looking down at her food. The man is doing the same thing in a new shot. Then the third shot in this sequence, the woman puts a pineapple in her mouth with her fork.
The woman eats and then in another shot the man eats while putting steak into his mouth, both still silent.
The woman gets up from her seat and grabs a green wine bottle and tries to pour it into the man's glass.
The man says "not for me, dear" after covering his glass with his hand.
The woman sits back down at her seat.
The woman makes clear her deflation at the refusal. The man says that he can drink at the moment after the woman expresses displeasure at the refusal for wine. The man continues eating his steak.
The woman chugs the wine from her wine glass.
The woman puts down the glass, which bumps into another glass and then there is a ding from the collision.
The woman, satisfied, pours herself more wine and places the bottle on the table with a "bomp" and a "ding" again. The man compliments the asparagus.
The woman says "They say it's an aphrodisiac."
The man asks "who does?"
The woman says "No one, that's just what people say."
The man finishes his bite and licks his lips.
The man asks "How dear, how was your day?"
The woman throws wine at the man, getting angry. She says "Shut up and have a drink. You're a computer, not a fucking surgeon."
The woman asks the man to "relax once in your life."
The man takes out a box of cigarettes and takes one out, and the woman says "Don't you dare smoke in my house" while the man continues to smoke.
The woman grabs the cigarette from the man's mouth and disposes of it, only for the man to light another one.
The woman sits back down saying, "Passing smoke. That's exactly what I wanted for my anniversary."
The woman complains about "stilted conversation" and "short mechanical sex" if she's really lucky.
The man and the woman stare at each other for a moment, both silent, both displeased at the other.
The woman says "I'm sorry" and gets up from her seat.
The woman goes to the man's lap to hug the man, and she says she's sorry and didn't mean what she said.
The two embrace for a moment, the woman hugging the man.
The woman slides her hand down the man's neck, and uncovers the power switch behind his neck
She looks displeased but turns the robot off by pulling down the switch behind the man's neck, revealing that he is a robot.
The woman settles the man down, and walks away.
The woman sits back down and takes off her shoes, putting her feet on the table, looking displeased with the robot.
The woman says "happy anniversary" in a chilling tone.
Pan up from a Mac laptop, the woman is typing on the computer with a bell-chiming tune behind it.
The woman is looking at the website for "Prometheus Robotics" for a replacement robot for the one she turned off just before.
The woman looks at the previous robot and likes a bearded robot man who has a scruffy look with unbuttoned shirts and longer hair.
The woman takes out a headset and dials the number "0800 800 800" with a dial-up sound.
Another woman answers saying her name is "Rachel" from "Technical Services" and asks how she may help.
The woman answers that she is unsatisfied with her current robot
The first woman answers that the model is "100 series" and that he lacks ambition.
The called woman answers that lack of ambition is a common malfunction of the 100 series.
The woman asks Rachel if she can something "a bit sportier."
Rachel asks for preferences.
The woman answers a rock climber then rejects that and asks for a massager. The she rejects that and asks for a "rock climbing massager." The camera pans out.
The woman asks that the man has no beard but maybe a five o'clock shadow.
The woman asks for shorter hair.
The woman asks for blond hair, and then some more features but then the camera fades to black as the music swells in the background.
There is knocking on the door in a black screen, then the woman peeks through the eyehole.
The woman opens the door, and there are two women from Prometheus Robotics, one carrying a pad and paper and the other carrying a robot.
They unwrap the new model's head. He is blond with a thin five o'clock shadow beard, and his face looks very close to Chris Pratt.
The first woman looks in shock and awe. The music swells as the camera closes up on her face.
The two Prometheus Robotics women take the old model out on a cart with some straps around it.
The camera pans away from the woman as she watches the old model go.
The woman asks for the two other women to stop for a moment, then kisses that man on the lips goodbye.
The woman takes the ring from the robot man's (old) hand.
She lifts the new man's hand.
She puts the ring on the ring finger.
The woman turns on the new robot man's power switch.
The robot is activated and becomes aware of its surroundings, booting up.
The robot grabs the fork and knife on front of him, at the seat where the previous man was originally.
The woman asks if the new man wants wine.
The man refuses the offer and responds that he has a big hike the next day.
The man responds that the asparagus is very tender.
The woman smiles and says "yes, dear."
The man says that it's for aphrodisiac, and says that it's just what people say, reversing what happened before.
The man gets up and walks over to the woman and walks behind her chair.
The man asks the woman about her day as she is being messaged by the new man, feeling comfortable about it.
The woman says that she went to pick up some stuff for tonight as man continues the massage, going for light lunch with Anna.
The woman says that she decided to treat herself since its their anniversary.
The man finds the woman's switch and turns her off the same way that was done before.
The man walks away with the woman's head having fallen over down and frozen in her stare.
The man walks over to the couch with a cigarette and his foot on the table, watching television while woman is turned off.
The man turns on sports on the television.
The final shot is of the man looking at the TV blankly, putting down his cigar.
Black screen, no music
Title "The Shoe" fades in and the words expand as they fade in
Fade into a black shoe sideways on a sidewalk with birds chirping and traffic going by
Title "How They Get Here" is seen in the top left corner with black text, the camera starts to shake a little and music starts playing
As the camera tilts downward and the music enters the trumpets with a comedic jazz theme, the shoe is actually being worn and the person wearing it sets his shoe upright on the sidewalk
The camera tilts downward even more and the person is tapping his shoe on the sidewalk
The camera changes to its side angle and the person is still tapping his shoe on the sidewalk.
The man drops a wrapper on the ground, a blue wrapper with some white and navy blue words on it.
The man, in a close-up of the milk carton mouth, opens up a red and white milk carton by pulling it forward and back at the area where the mouth is
The man smiles looking down as he is leaning on a car in a nice neighborhood in a new camera angle
The man looks into the milk carton and the camera shot is placed at the mouth of the milk carton.
The man drinks the milk carton then looks around as the camera is still in the same shot as was on his face and then looks around again after drinking for a second time
The man notices a brown-haired woman is walking down the street in a red shirt, black skirt, black shoes.
The man, wearing a brown jacket and jeans, sneakers, and a collared shirt, leans on the car as a mustached man walks over to the car, and the camera revolves around the man
The mustached man snaps at the milk drinking man who jumps off leaning on the car after being surprised by the mustached man.
The milk drinking man walks away from the car and trips on sidewalk as he runs off and walks on the sidewalk as the camera follows the milk drinking man
The man notices the woman across the street as the camera follows
The woman mimics the man tripping on the sidewalk as the camera follows
The man notices and is confused at first at the woman's actions as he continues walking across the sidewalk
The man puts the milk carton on a white fence and continues to walk as the camera still follows him walking down the sidewalk.
The woman mimics the man again putting the milk carton on the fence as they walk down the street

The woman speeds up a little along her walk on the sidewalk beside a big brick building and then looks at the man across the street, camera following. There are two people who walk by her: two darker-skinned individuals, one in a white, long-sleeved shirt and shorts with a red hat and another with jeans and a blue sweater.
The man does a dance by swinging his arms as he continues down the street along a large white fence with some cars behind it
The woman does the same thing as she walks down the path with the big brick building behind her
They continue to do different dances mimicking each other doing the swinging arms dance
The woman passes by a sign for a building and starts to dance like a chicken
The man mimics the woman and does the same dance, both continuing down the sidewalk
They both end up near a street crossing as they both mimic each other faking aching bodies down the street
Woman is shocked at a car coming at the man and the man mimics her as the car is coming behind him.
Woman is trying to tell him to notice the car and get him to stop, but the man still does not notice.
A maroon car hits the man and the man is flying on top of the car due to the impact.
The woman tries not to look.
The car turns and sparks are flying, and it drives off into the air by driving up a car carrier
Car goes flying into the car in different angles
Woman watches the car flying in shock with her hands over her mouth
The car makes impact with the ground, smoke everywhere, and the windshield breaks as well as the front of the hood.
Woman looks up as a black shoe is flying in the air by some telephone poles and transformers.
The shoe lands next to the sidewalk as the camera passes it by
The camera stops at another black shoe on the side of a sidewalk, near some weeds and moss.
The camera stays on the other shoe for a few seconds then it snaps to black
The camera stays on the other shoe for a few seconds then it snaps to black
In a wide shot we see a car drive up to a very tall building where everything except the car is in shadow.
The car runs its engine at the house for a second.
Cuts to title in white text over black screen: "Keith Reynolds can't make it tonight"
A blue stick figure stop the car and then gets out of the car and finally shuts the door to his car.
The blue stick figure walks up to the porch of the building.
The blue stick figure goes back to his car and puts his black hat on and then closes the door.
The blue figure walks up to the building as the music plays and then the narrator introduces the blue figure as "Keith Reynolds" and that today is "Promotion Day"

The blue figure walks inside the office where a red and a gray figure are talking with each other while the narrator states that the blue man is a financial analyst, and then runs into the elevator with an orange figure.
The blue figure's elevator goes up to a top floor and walk in as the narrator continues talking about a potential promotion.
The blue figure walks into a room with a pink person in the room as the narrator states that his promotion would make the pink person, Sarah, like Keith.
Keith goes to the coffee machine and grabs some coffee after putting his briefcase down.
Keith gives a coffee to Sarah, but Sarah is confused. The narrator says that this happens every day and Sarah never wants coffee.
Keith walks away from Sarah and grabs the suitcase and heads out the door.
A man falls off the side of a building, an orange man.
The grey person looks outside his window after getting up from the desk.
The grey person looks outside and the narrator confirms that someone has fallen. The grey person gets the phone and calls an ambulance.
Keith takes the elevator up to a floor where a green man and a red man are shaking hands.
Keith walks in and finds the green man is walking away from the elevator.
The green man takes the elevator down
The green man takes the elevator down to Sarah's level and straightens his tie and walks into Sarah's floor. His name is Patrick Campbell.
Keith shakes his boss's hand and the handshake was weaker than expected. The boss sits in chair while Keith stands up. The promotion was given to the green man, Patrick.
Patrick is telling Sarah that he got the promotion.
Keith takes the elevator back to Sarah's level.
The doors open and Keith sees Patrick and Sarah kissing.
Keith drops his suitcase and just stares.
Patrick walks into the elevator after saying goodbye to Sarah.
Both Patrick and Keith take the elevator up.
Patrick walks out of the elevator and Keith curses at Patrick
Patrick walks back in, Keith apologizes, Patrick pulls the suitcase down, and then Patrick punches Keith in the face
Keith fights back and beats up Patrick with blood sprayed in the elevator.
Keith has killed Patrick with his suitcase.
Keith is shocked at what he has done.
Ruth, a mustard green woman stick figure, from Accounting needs the toilet and decides to take the stairs due to her New Years Resolution to get to the one with the nice soap dispenser.
Keith pulls the body from the elevator.
Keith pulls the body down the stairs while Ruth is going up, and they meet.
They stare at each other for a moment.
Ruth passes out down the stairs, dead from impact.
Keith tries to not step over Ruth's body.
Sarah grabs some files to see Patrick again.
Keith climbs off the stairs and then stops and sees a ladder.
Keith heads towards the ladder.
A red guy at a higher floor throws a paper ball into a basket and misses.
Sarah sees Keith and drops the files and stare at each other for a moment.
They stare at each other for a moment.
Sarah faints.
Keith gets over the window and pulls the body.
Keith drops Patrick's body out the window, and hangs onto a pole.
Keith realizes that he just wants what everyone wants: a job, family, etc.
He hangs on and realizes that a single moment can redefine your life.
He looks at someone he never saw before as his life crumbles before his eyes.
Barry looks at Keith, who's looking at him. Then they cut to black.
Song "Let's All Go To The Lobby" plays as cartoon characters march down to the lobby.
The characters sing about popcorn while the popcorn dispenser is going wild.
There are people eating candy and drinking beverages while the aforementioned characters sing about it.
The camera pans away from the concessions aisle of the theater as the characters conclude the song "Let's All Go To The Lobby."
The characters hold that last note as a green screen and counter for the movie counts down from 3 to 1 and the movie starts.
The title "The Rock" in all capital letters in white text over a black background fades in and expands then fades out.
We see an animated underwater scene, where a mollusk pushes to the left of the camera while there are starfish and seaweed as well as dirt with some squids in the back.
The camera pans up with some fish passing by to the land.
On land, some birds fly by, and the mountain blinks for a bit.
A dragonfly stands, spazzes, and then flies to the left.
A lizard-fish gets up and walks on a rock after peeking out. He screams then jumps back into the water.
Pan up to the big mountain and he looks sad.
The mountain sleeps through time, closing his eyes, with dinosaurs running and mountains forming.
The mountain is engulfed in a glacier in the Ice Age, buried under the ground.
Trees and hills and grass start growing, and the Mountain has been submerged.
The Mountain hears hits and it's waking him up. Zoom out and it's a lumberjack cutting down a tree.
The man walks past the Mountain and the Mountain goes to sleep.
He hears more smacking and zoom out, it's man settling over that land.
The Rock pops out of the ground and walks to the left after scratching his back.
The Rock finds a spot with some leaves and then takes a nap on the leaves next to a big rock formation.
The Rock is sleeping but a man and his horses wrap a rope around his nose and pull the rock down.
The Rock is taken by carriage across the plains.
The Rock is used as part of a device, as some sort of wheel with a rope around the nose.
The device seems to be something that pulls in the river water and pull the salt.
The Rock is spun really fast after some salt is deposited.
Some guards or officials in blue uniforms with muskets come and take the Rock away.
The guards crush the Rock and graze him into a sphere to be part of another device.
A man rings a bell and whistles with two other guards. This man has yellow gloves, a blond beard, and a black cowboy hat.
The Rock is being used as a cannonball to destroy the ship of the Confederate ship during the Civil War, breaking through a pole.
The Rock is broken and falls close to the water with a bunch of fish and seaweed and sad nearby.
A wind blows the Rock away to the left under the sea.
The Rock washes up by a coastal side, and then is washed back further into land.
The Rock walks by a bill with a man operating a tractor grazing over it for settled land.
The Rock finds a glove and pulls back the fingers to use a bed to sleep in.
A man in glasses and a brown goatee with glasses and a brown coat grabs the Rock and dusts it off and turns it around, revealing a fossil.
The man laughs weirdly as he puts the Rock in a red toolbox and prances away, still laughing.
A crowd watches as a photographer takes a picture of the Rock, who is on display with other fossils in a museum.
We see the Rock is in a museum with other fossils like that of dinosaurs with other fossils with rocks with eyes.
A kid hits the glass, knocking the Rock backwards behind the curtain down a pipe.
The Rock falls into the trash bin of a janitor, who looks surprised that he heard a noise, but continues moving.
The Rock is thrown out into the street, only to kicked by a passerby who's holding a bouquet of flowers and is in a red and brown striped shirt, disgruntled.
The man throws the Rock into the window of an apartment building, hitting another man inside the apartment building,
The Rock rolls down the apartment building onto a train track and then is thrown out into traffic, thrown into one of the car's gears.
The Rock is crushed in the gears and falls out and then grabs a paper to cover himself.
The Rock looks up to see a giant steamroller coming towards him and rolling over and making him much smaller.
The Rock has fallen into a sack of trash and the sac is swept out by a man in a green shirt and blue overalls, falling onto a beach.
The Rock climbs over the trash and grabs a piece of fruit to sleep on, and then closes his eyes.
The Rock's pile of trash is excavated by an excavator and that excavator puts that into a dump truck.
The Rock is in a cart that is heading towards a melting chamber.
The Rock falls into the melting chamber after falling off the assembly line and the screen turns to white.
The Rock has been made into a glass disk, and two people in lighter blue hazmat suits cover the Rock in a dark blue box.
A white, black, and red rocket ship launches from Earth to outer space.
The rocket disassembles and the satellite transforms out into the required shape. The control center claps for the rocket and one guy presses a big red button.
The Rock is the glass front of the satellite, and the satellite rockets into an asteroid and it gets bent out of shape.
The satellite heads towards a new planet and the Rock and the satellite burn up into the atmosphere, then makes impact.
The Rock becomes a new mountain on this new planet as it becomes a new atmosphere with new mountains and water as well as life.
The Rock pauses and looks around, then finds peace on this new world.
The Rock takes a deep breath and that creates a wind.
The movie fades to black.
The title "The Prisoner" fades in and expands in white text over a black background and fades away.
Fade into a scene with a cafe where a man walks in with a black suit.
The man asks the name of this place after the waitress says that the place will be open soon. The waitress responds with "you're new here, aren't you?"
The waitress asks if the man wants breakfast.
The man asks where the police station is.
The woman responds with "there isn't one." Man asks for a phone.
Woman responds that there's a phone box around the corner.
The man walks down the street around the corner.
The man stops and looks around then keeps going.
The man walks up to the phone, and takes a look at the phone and presses a button to turn it on.
The man tries to call but he cannot call because of "no number, no call" after a series of confusing questions to call.
The man hangs up the phone and places it back on the table.
The man walks down the street and looks around.
The man stops to look around then turns to his left and walks in that direction to a map.

The man looks at the map for a moment. The map is itself green but there are different buttons, such as a high-number keypad. The man examines the different numbers to call, and his finger lands at "taxi rank," which is number "9" on the keypad.
The man presses "9" on the number keyboard, but then a motorcycle noise starts revving.
A woman in a red striped shirt stops the car, indicating that she was the source of the call. The woman asks "where to, sir?" The man is startled a bit.
She says the same in French or Spanish, indicating that this is in Europe.
The man responds that he wants to go to the "nearest town," but she says that her service only local.
The man responds that he wants to go as far as she is able to go.

They drive down the street and the man asks why she spoke to him in French, and then she says that the language is international, but then he responds that it is probably a waste of time to ask where they are.
The man responds to the woman saying she thought the man was Polish or Czech, saying what would someone who'd be Polish or Czech be doing "out here."
The woman responds that you never who you'll meet next.
They drive down the road, and they pass by some nice houses, all the while the man having a worried expression and the woman smiling at him.
She drives down further, stopping near a white building, saying that she is only local.
The woman yells that the charge is two units at the man as he gets out.
The man asks "units?" The woman says "credit units." She then says to the man that to pay her next time.
The man walks back as the taxi drives away. He walks into the nearest building to the left of him.
The man walks into a "general store," which has a hanging sign saying as much. He walks into the building.

The man walks into the general store, which has a lot of flowers and candies at the front of its entrance. He walks in slowly, unsure of what to expect. There is a voice that is speaking a language that is not English.
It turns out to be the owner of the store, and the owner asks a customer to help herself to a pineapple.
